mnemonics: HOW: Hold Or Wait (what the mask does to signals)
keyPoints: Used to examine or change the calling process's signal mask, which is the set of signals currently blocked from delivery.
It takes three arguments: 'how' (SIG_BLOCK, SIG_UNBLOCK, SIG_SETMASK), 'set' (the new signal set), and 'oldset' (to save the previous mask).
Blocking a signal is different from ignoring it; a blocked signal is not delivered until it is unblocked, while an ignored signal is delivered and then dismissed.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to initialize the signal set with sigemptyset() or sigfillset() before using it, leading to undefined behavior.
Assuming sigprocmask() permanently blocks a signal; it only blocks signals for the duration of the process's current mask, which can be changed later.
Confusing the 'how' operations, especially mixing up SIG_BLOCK (adds signals to the current mask) and SIG_SETMASK (replaces the entire current mask).
